name: Principal Baldev Singh (Delhi)
body: Principal Baldev Singh was one of the leading figures in Gurmat Sangeet of his time. He was born on 4th June 1944 in the home of Sardar Teja Singh Ji. He went on to learn Gurmat Sangeet from Professor Rajinder Singh who was the Principal of the Shaheed Sikh Missionary College, Amritsar. He completed his course within 3 years but he continued to hone his skills for many years after graduating to achieve greater heights.  In his interview with ETC Punjabi, Principal Baldev Singh Ji spoke about his daily ‘riyaz’ routine which took up approximately 10 hours a day and he continued this daily 10 hour routine for 10 years.

His first duty as Head Raagi was in 1960 in Takht Sri Keshgarh Sahib, Anandpur Sahib. Subsequently, Principal Baldev Singh Ji went on to serve Gurdwara Dukh Nivaran Sahib Patiala, Sri Tarn Taran Sahib and Mukatsar Sahib for short periods of time. From around 1967 to 1974, he served as Hazoori Raagi in Sri Harmandir Sahib, Amritsar. During that time, Sant Kartar Singh (Bhindra Wale) was running the Sri Guru Granth Sahib Gurmat Sangeet Vidhyala and he requested Bhai Baldev Singh Ji to take on the role of Principal of the Vidhyala which he did for a few years. Principal Baldev Singh Ji finally came to settle in Delhi around 1980 where he served as Head Raagi for many years. During that time he also used to conduct interviews for jathas applying for the position of Hazoori Raagis in the Gurdwaras of Delhi.

Principal Baldev Singh Ji has many awards to his name and the first award he received was in his early teenage years in 1957 where he got second place in a Sikh Educational Conference by Chief Khalsa Diwan, Jalandhar.  In 1962, he won first prize in a Classical Music competition organised by All India Radio. The government of Punjab also awarded him the first Bhai Mardana Award in 1969 during a Samagam held in conjunction with the 500 year Prakash Purab of Guru Nanak Dev Ji. He was conferred the award of Shiromani Raagi as well in 2001. Unfortunately, due to complications of uncontrolled diabetes, Principal Baldev Singh Ji passed away in January 2006. The above information was condensed from the pages of ‘Gurmat Sangeet De Anmol Ratan’ written by late Bhai Nirmal Singh Khalsa’. If anyone has more recordings of Principal Baldev Singh Ji, please contact us.

The following recordings were contributed by Pertahp Singh Tuli from Toronto, Canada. Pertahp Singh also runs the SikhVibes page on Soundcloud. Please follow his page at the following link for more wonderful recordings https://soundcloud.com/sikhvibes
